# Report { #pycmdcheck.Report }

```python
Report(results=list(), package_path='')
```

Collection of check results for a package.

A Report aggregates multiple CheckResult objects and provides
methods for querying the overall status and summarizing results.

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**results**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)\[[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   List of CheckResult objects from running checks.

<code>[**package_path**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Path to the package that was checked.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

Create a report and add results:

```python
>>> report = Report(package_path="/path/to/package")
>>> report.add(CheckResult("metadata", CheckStatus.OK, "Valid"))
>>> report.add(CheckResult("tests", CheckStatus.OK, "Passed"))
>>> report.passed
True
```

Check for specific failure conditions:

```python
>>> report.failed_on(["error"])
False
>>> report.failed_on(["error", "warning"])
False
```

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [add](#pycmdcheck.Report.add) | Add a check result to the report. |
| [count_by_status](#pycmdcheck.Report.count_by_status) | Count results by status. |
| [failed_on](#pycmdcheck.Report.failed_on) | Check if any result matches the fail_on criteria. |
| [to_dict](#pycmdcheck.Report.to_dict) | Convert to dictionary for JSON serialization. |

### add { #pycmdcheck.Report.add }

```python
Report.add(result)
```

Add a check result to the report.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**result**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The CheckResult to add.

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> report = Report()
>>> report.add(CheckResult("test", CheckStatus.OK, "Passed"))
>>> len(report.results)
1
```

### count_by_status { #pycmdcheck.Report.count_by_status }

```python
Report.count_by_status()
```

Count results by status.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[CheckStatus](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckStatus`), [int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A dictionary mapping each CheckStatus to the count of results

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[CheckStatus](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckStatus`), [int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   with that status.

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> report = Report()
>>> report.add(CheckResult("a", CheckStatus.OK, "OK"))
>>> report.add(CheckResult("b", CheckStatus.OK, "OK"))
>>> report.add(CheckResult("c", CheckStatus.WARNING, "Warn"))
>>> counts = report.count_by_status()
>>> counts[CheckStatus.OK]
2
>>> counts[CheckStatus.WARNING]
1
```

### failed_on { #pycmdcheck.Report.failed_on }

```python
Report.failed_on(fail_on)
```

Check if any result matches the fail_on criteria.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**fail_on**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)\[[str](`str`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   List of status values to consider as failures (e.g., ["error", "warning"]).

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[bool](`bool`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   True if any result has a status in the fail_on list.

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> report = Report()
>>> report.add(CheckResult("test", CheckStatus.WARNING, "Warn"))
>>> report.failed_on(["error"])
False
>>> report.failed_on(["error", "warning"])
True
```

### to_dict { #pycmdcheck.Report.to_dict }

```python
Report.to_dict()
```

Convert to dictionary for JSON serialization.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [Any](`typing.Any`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A dictionary containing:

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [Any](`typing.Any`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - package_path: The path that was checked

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [Any](`typing.Any`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - passed: Whether all checks passed

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [Any](`typing.Any`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - summary: Count of each status

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [Any](`typing.Any`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - results: List of result dictionaries

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> report = Report(package_path="/pkg")
>>> report.add(CheckResult("test", CheckStatus.OK, "OK"))
>>> data = report.to_dict()
>>> data["passed"]
True
>>> data["summary"]["ok"]
1
```
